steps.title: Find the shared explanation
steps.content: Both explanations account for the same bacterial genera appearing in leaves and roots. Soil bacteria might reach leaves through rain splash, or windborne bacteria might reach both aboveground parts and soil or roots.

steps.title: Infer the common source
steps.content: A compares benefits, which the passage never discusses. C reflects only the wind possibility and only leaves. D introduces harmful bacteria without support. Since each explanation describes bacteria moving between plant areas from a shared origin, common source is the supported inference. The answer is B.
